WI-4.notes: Authoritative prior production proof remains valid; no new single-run execution was required in this rerun.

WI-5.notes: March 30 completed the real delegated DNS + ACM path, production create run ff6dea42-dbdc-42f1-996f-da437ad48e4c, truthful local apply with CloudFront distribution E3VUPASYW2QL80 and OAC EDLL3VYQR916E, recompute retention, and exact rollback back to the original website, bucket policy, and all-false bucket-level PAB baseline.

WI-9.notes: Full truthful production create/apply/rollback proof retained under scenarios/wi9/ with exact pre-state restoration.

WI-10.notes: Full truthful production public-policy-scrub proof retained under scenarios/wi10/, including approved temporary account-level S3 Public Access Block disablement, seeded public policy, final create/apply/rollback proof, and restoration of both the bucket baseline and the original all-true account-level S3 Public Access Block settings.

WI-11.notes: Authoritative prior production proof remains valid; the preferred grouped S3.11 attempt in this rerun failed only because the live group still contained deleted buckets.

preferred_s3_11_attempt.notes: Bundle executed 19 executable members, but 5 deleted buckets caused truthful NoSuchBucket failures and the callback finalized the group run as failed.

fallback_s3_2_attempt.notes: Mixed-tier grouped bundle finalized successfully with 2 executable members and 8 metadata-only members; both executable members were rolled back to exact pre-run baseline.

environment_mutations: Control-plane freshness for account 696505809372 was refreshed through the production synthetic-event path before live scenario execution.

Disposable Route53 hosted zone Z089211911JQN783YLH5I and imported test certificate arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:696505809372:certificate/e24f54d8-3a83-4de9-88d4-1dcc3cb9b8eb were created for the WI-5 attempt and then deleted after the CloudFront certificate failure.

March 30 completed the real WI-5 prerequisite path: Route53 hosted zone Z081885955B6GUR4IL7E for wi5-gate3-696505809372.ocypheris.com was publicly delegated from ocypheris.com, and Amazon-issued ACM certificate arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:696505809372:certificate/509785ff-eef1-4071-bddc-d275f1dfa6ed reached ISSUED.

WI-5 run ff6dea42-dbdc-42f1-996f-da437ad48e4c applied successfully, creating CloudFront distribution E3VUPASYW2QL80, OAC EDLL3VYQR916E, Route53 A/AAAA aliases to dxxm59bao95gt.cloudfront.net, website disablement, and bucket-level Public Access Block enforcement.

WI-5 rollback destroyed the generated CloudFront, OAC, and alias records, then manually restored the captured S3 website configuration, the original two-statement bucket policy, and the baseline bucket-level Public Access Block configuration with all four flags false.
Grouped S3.11 lifecycle changes were applied to 14 live buckets and then removed after the preferred grouped attempt failed on stale deleted members.
Grouped S3.2 fallback applied two executable members and both were fully rolled back, including CloudFront distribution E2P5LVIEQSCKI7 and OAC E5PPLWFN6FAQK.

WI-10 temporarily required account-level S3 Public Access Block to be disabled on account 696505809372 so the public-policy-scrub candidate could be seeded truthfully; the original all-true account-level baseline was restored after proof.

WI-10 surfaced two current-head bundle regressions in production, both fixed minimally in backend/services/pr_bundle.py and redeployed on image tags 20260329T222719Z and 20260329T224217Z before the final corrected rerun succeeded.
